column2: The person ultimately accountable for data, responsible for defining classification and authorizing access.
The person responsible for technical implementation of security controls and day-to-day data maintenance.
US Government classification for information whose disclosure causes "exceptionally grave damage."
US Government classification for information whose disclosure causes "serious damage."
US Government classification for information whose disclosure causes "damage."
The cost to replace an asset at current market prices; used in BCP/DR planning.
The indirect loss (revenue, reputation) resulting from an asset's failure.
Unauthorized hardware or software used within an organization without IT's knowledge.
Role focused on data quality, metadata, and consistency across the organization.
Principle stating a system must be protected at the level of its most sensitive data component.
Removing inapplicable controls from a security baseline.
Refining and adjusting baseline controls to meet specific organizational needs.
The physical labeling of media (folders, CDs) to indicate its classification.
The digital metadata applied to files to indicate their classification.
A comprehensive list of assets, including owner, criticality, and classification.
Controlled Unclassified Information; a US government label for sensitive but unclassified data.
An asset without physical substance, such as brand reputation or intellectual property.
The process of moving data to a lower classification level when its sensitivity decreases.
Commercial data containing trade secrets or sensitive business information.
The principle that users should only have access to data required for their specific tasks.
The individual to whom the personal data refers.
An entity that processes data on behalf of a Data Controller.
Information intended for unrestricted distribution with no impact if disclosed.
Commercial classification for data not for public release but with low impact if lost.
The process of assigning a value to an asset to determine appropriate protection levels.
